name: Aznalcazar and Ribera Rio Guadiamar — circular through the village and surroundings
name_original: Aznalcázar y ribera Río Guadiamar — circular por el pueblo y alrededores
region_intro: Dog friendly hiking trails in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar traverse a diverse landscape shaped by the Guadiamar River, acting as a vital ecological connection between Sierra Morena and Doñana National Park. The region features a mosaic of Mediterranean forests, dehesas, agricultural lands, and riverine ecosystems with dense gallery forests. As the river approaches Doñana, the terrain transitions to sandy areas, pine forests, and extensive marshlands. This protected landscape offers varied terrain for hikers and their dogs. Best dog friendly hiking trails in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar The most popular dog friendly hiking route is El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail, a 6.9 miles (11.2 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 8 minutes to complete, winding through varied landscapes of Mediterranean forest and open fields. Another top favourite among local hikers is Path Between Stone Walls – Scenic Rocky Path loop from El Castillo de las Guardas, a moderate 5.1 miles (8.3 km) path. This route offers views of the surrounding dehesas and agricultural lands. Local hikers also love the Castillo de las Guardas – Scenic Rocky Path loop from El Castillo de las Guardas, a 9.3 miles (14.9 km) trail leading through Mediterranean woodlands and open pastures, often completed in about 4 hours 18 minutes. Hiking in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar is defined by the Guadiamar River, Mediterranean forests, dehesas, and marshlands. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult trails, catering to various experience levels. The routes in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 150 reviews. More than 750 hikers have used komoot to explore Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many dog-friendly hiking trails are available in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar offers a good selection of dog-friendly trails, with 16 routes specifically curated for you and your canine companion. These range from easy strolls to more challenging hikes, ensuring there's something for every fitness level.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on dog-friendly hikes in this region?
A: The Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar features diverse terrain, from Mediterranean forests and dehesas near Sierra Morena to agricultural lands, riverine ecosystems, and even pine forests and marshlands closer to Doñana. Many sections follow old railway lines or riverbanks, offering generally flat and accessible paths suitable for dogs. You'll find a mosaic of landscapes as you explore the river's course.

Q: Are there any easy dog-friendly routes suitable for beginners or older dogs?
A: Yes, there are 3 easy dog-friendly routes in the area. These trails are generally flatter and shorter, perfect for a relaxed walk with your dog. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010559049/el-castillo-de-las-guardas-las-canadillas-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Castillo de las Guardas - Las Cañadillas Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate option that can be adapted for a gentler pace.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see with my dog along the trails?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and points of interest. You can explore the diverse ecosystems of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3987366" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiamar Green Corridor Protected Landscape&lt;/a&gt; itself, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5393447" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de las Doblas&lt;/a&gt;, an artificial lagoon with a wooden walkway, great for birdwatching. The historical &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1824240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vado del Quema Shrine&lt;/a&gt; is another notable cultural landmark you might encounter.

Q: Are there any loop trails for dog walking in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: Yes, several dog-friendly loop trails are available, offering convenient circular routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621369" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Path Between Stone Walls – Scenic Rocky Path loop from El Castillo de las Guardas&lt;/a&gt;, which provides a scenic circular hike without needing to retrace your steps.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike with my dog in this region?
A: The Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar is pleasant for hiking with dogs during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot, so if you visit then, it's best to hike early in the morning or late in the afternoon, and ensure your dog has plenty of water and shade. Winter offers cooler temperatures, making it suitable for longer walks.

Q: Are there specific rules or considerations for dogs on the trails?
A: While the trails are dog-friendly, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially given the rich biodiversity and wildlife in the area, which includes various birds and mammals like foxes and otters. This helps protect both your dog and the local fauna. Always carry waste bags and clean up after your pet.

Q: Where can I find parking for dog-friendly hikes?
A: Many trailheads in the Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar, particularly around towns like El Castillo de las Guardas, offer designated parking areas. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21846020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Guardas – El Castillo de las Guardas loop from Bar Casa Juanito&lt;/a&gt; often has parking available near local establishments or trail access points.

Q: What do other hikers say about the dog-friendly trails in Corredor Ecológico Del Río Guadiamar?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from 157 ratings. Reviewers often praise the peaceful natural surroundings, the diverse landscapes, and the accessibility of many paths, making it a wonderful place to explore with dogs.

Q: Are there any longer dog-friendly routes for a full day out?
A: For a longer adventure, consider routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20121658" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de las Guardas – Scenic Rocky Path loop from El Castillo de las Guardas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans nearly 15 km. These longer trails allow you and your dog to immerse yourselves more deeply in the varied ecosystems of the Guadiamar Green Corridor.

Q: Is there good water access for dogs along the trails?
A: Given that the corridor follows the Guadiamar River, there are opportunities for water access, particularly in the riverine ecosystems. However, it's always best to carry sufficient water for your dog, especially during warmer months, as natural water sources can vary and may not always be suitable for drinking.
tips: The parish church of San Pablo is located in the Plaza de la Iglesia, near the Old Fountain and the El Arquillo Cultural Center, in the Seville town of Aznalcázar. This building is one of the most beautiful representative works of the Sevillian Mudejar XIV century.
